sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system documentation available for NES version

special_mechanics.name: Vehicle-specific handling
special_mechanics.description: Different vehicle types (formula one cars, powerboats, helicopters, tanks) with distinct handling characteristics. Tanks can shoot other racers.

special_mechanics.name: Track boundary enforcement
special_mechanics.description: If a player leaves the defined race route for too long, they are sent back to the track

special_mechanics.name: Obstacle interaction
special_mechanics.description: Courses contain obstacles such as cereal boxes on breakfast tables and pencil sharpeners on desks

tier_notes: Tier 3: Empirical/qualitative data only. Micro Machines is a top-down racing game featuring toy vehicles in household environments. No physics parameters are documented in available sources. Game mechanics focus on track navigation, vehicle-specific handling, and competitive multiplayer rather than detailed movement physics. Development notes indicate the team used reverse-engineered NES knowledge and basic AI with invisible directional arrows.
sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Micro Machines (NES)
